candidate_problems.text: How can 'closure under self-reference' be made precise enough to distinguish genuinely life-like organizational self-production from ordinary autocatalysis, homeostasis, or dissipative self-organization? The conjecture hinges on a threshold concept, but it is unresolved what measurable network properties, causal criteria, or dynamical invariants would mark that transition.

Can a distributed, organization-level replicator undergo open-ended Darwinian evolution before sequence-based templating exists? The key tension is whether whole-network or protocell-level strange loops can achieve heredity, variation, and differential persistence with sufficient fidelity, or whether localized informational polymers are required much earlier than the conjecture allows.

By what concrete mechanisms can higher-order chemical organization 'reproduce its own constraints' in realistic prebiotic environments rather than only in idealized models? This remains open because compartments, catalysts, gradients, and environmental cycles may scaffold recurrence, but it is unclear how such feedback loops become robust, individuated, and experimentally realizable under geochemically plausible conditions.
